heading: Special authority for provision of space launch support services to increase space launch capacity
label: 2276a Special authority for provision of space launch support services to increase space launch capacity
notes: Amendments2025—Subsecs. (c) to (e). added subsec. (c) and redesignated former subsecs. (c) and (d) as (d) and (e), respectively.Pub. L. 119–60
amendments: Amendments2025—Subsecs. (c) to (e). added subsec. (c) and redesignated former subsecs. (c) and (d) as (d) and (e), respectively.Pub. L. 119–60

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary of a military department may support Federal and commercial space launch capacity on any domestic real property under the control of the Secretary through the provision of space launch support services.

children.heading: Provision of Launch Equipment and Services to Commercial Entities.—
children.label: b Provision of Launch Equipment and Services to Commercial Entities.—

children.heading: .—Contract or other transaction authority
children.label: 1 .—Contract or other transaction authority
children.text: The Secretary of a military department may enter into a contract or other transaction with one or more commercial entities that intend to conduct space launch activities on a military installation under the jurisdiction of the Secretary. Under such a contract or agreement, the Secretary may agree to provide to the commercial entity supplies, services, equipment, and construction needed for commercial space launch.

children.heading: .—Direct costs
children.label: A .—Direct costs
children.text: If the Secretary of a military department enters into a contract or other transaction with a commercial entity under paragraph (1), such contract or transaction shall include a provision that requires the commercial entity to reimburse the Department of Defense for all direct costs to the United States that are associated with any good, service, or equipment provided to the commercial entity under the contract or other transaction.

children.heading: .—Indirect costs
children.label: B .—Indirect costs
children.text: If the Secretary of a military department enters into a contract or other transaction with a commercial entity under paragraph (1), such contract or transaction may include a provision that requires the commercial entity to reimburse the Department of Defense for such indirect costs as the Secretary considers to be appropriate. In such a case, such contract or other transaction may provide for the reimbursement of such indirect costs through the establishment of a rate, fixed price, or similar mechanism the Secretary concerned determines is reasonable.

children.heading: .—Retention of funds collected from commercial users
children.label: 3 .—Retention of funds collected from commercial users
children.text: Any amount collected from a commercial entity as a reimbursement under paragraph (2) shall be credited to the appropriations account from which the cost for which such reimbursement is provided was derived.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: 4 .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary of each of the military departments shall prescribe regulations to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: .—Noise Mitigation at Space Force Launch Sites
children.label: c .—Noise Mitigation at Space Force Launch Sites

children.text: study the noise caused by launches at launch sites of the Space Force; and
identify potential technologies and procedures to mitigate such noise to the extent practicable.

children.heading: .—Space launch
children.label: 1 .—Space launch
children.text: The term “space launch” includes all activities, supplies, equipment, facilities, or services supporting launch preparation, launch, reentry, recovery, and other launch-related activities for both the payload and the space transportation vehicle.

children.heading: .—Commercial entity
children.label: 2 .—Commercial entity
children.text: The term “commercial entity” or “commercial” means a non-Federal entity organized under the laws of the United States or of any jurisdiction within the United States.

with respect to any contract or other transaction authority entered into pursuant to subsection (b), limit the amount of the indirect costs that are reimbursable under paragraph (2)(B) of such subsection to not more than 30 percent, not to exceed $5,000,000 annually (based on fiscal year 2024 constant dollars), of the total amount of the direct costs reimbursable under paragraph (2)(A) of such subsection; and

children.label: A
children.text: an identification of the total amounts of direct and indirect costs reimbursed to each spaceport for the fiscal year covered by the report;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the support provided by reimbursed indirect costs for the fiscal year covered by the report; and

children.label: C
children.text: an identification of the rate, fixed price, or similar mechanism, if any, used to calculate the amount of the indirect costs that are reimbursable for the fiscal year following the fiscal year covered by the report.
